title: Psammogeton cabulicus
taxonomy: Psammogeton cabulicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to the Himalayas. The classification of Psammogeton cabulicus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Apiales, order Apiales, family Apiaceae, genus Psammogeton, species P. cabulicus. Psammogeton cabulicus is further classified into the subsection Apiaceae subfamily Apioideae. The family Apiaceae is also known as the carrot or parsley family.
uses: Psammogeton cabulicus, also known as Himalayan sandwort, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help in treating wounds and skin infections. Its extracts have been used to treat fever and other respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and constipation. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Psammogeton cabulicus for medicinal use.

properties: Psammogeton cabulicus has been found to have potential therapeutic properties, including its ability to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Research suggests that extracts from this plant may help in the treatment of various health conditions, such as liver and kidney disorders, as well as certain types of cancer.
